text: Strict tapers (progressively decreasing training volume each week) are associated with better marathon performance than relaxed tapers that do not progressively decrease volume.
Longer tapers of up to 3 weeks are associated with better marathon performance than shorter tapers.
A strict 3-week taper is associated with a median finish-time saving of 5 minutes 32 seconds (2.6%) compared with a minimal taper.
Female runners experience greater finish-time benefits than male runners for a given taper type of 3 weeks or shorter duration.
Most recreational marathon runners (64%) adopt less disciplined 2-week or 3-week tapers rather than strict progressive tapers.
Taper findings for recreational runners are consistent with studies on highly-trained athletes, where disciplined tapers produce comparable performance benefits.
